response:
THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES IS CALCULATED BY SUMMING THE AREAS OF EACH CONCENTRIC CIRCLE. THE AREA OF A CIRCLE IS πR², WHERE R IS THE RADIUS OF THE CIRCLE. THE RADIUS OF EACH SUBSEQUENT CIRCLE IS 10% LARGER THAN THE PREVIOUS, SO THE RADIUS OF THE NTH CIRCLE IS r × (1.1)^(N-1). THE TOTAL AREA IS THE SUM OF πR² FOR N = 1 TO 8. THIS IS A GEOMETRIC SERIES WITH FIRST TERM πr² AND COMMON RATIO 1.1. THE SUM OF A GEOMETRIC SERIES IS (a₁(1 - rⁿ)) / (1 - r), WHERE a₁ IS THE FIRST TERM AND r IS THE COMMON RATIO. SUBSTITUTING THE VALUES, THE TOTAL AREA IS πr² × (1 - (1.1)^8) / (1 - 1.1). THIS IS THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES.

THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS DETERMINED BY DIVIDING THE CIRCUMFERENCE BY THE GAP BETWEEN CHARACTERS. THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF A CIRCLE IS 2πR, WHERE R IS THE RADIUS OF THE CIRCLE. THE 5TH CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS OF r × (1.1)^4. THE CIRCUMFERENCE IS 2πr × (1.1)^4. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS THE CIRCUMFERENCE DIVIDED BY THE GAP BETWEEN CHARACTERS, WHICH IS 0.5 CM. THIS GIVES THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS AS (2πr × (1.1)^4) / 0.5. THIS IS THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE.
